objectives: Review taxonomy integrity of item 13.3 软件架构风格

taxonomy_suspect_context.original_suspicion: Item 13.3 was flagged during Phase 6.0 planning as a taxonomy suspect due to potential structural ambiguity or boundary misalignment. Expansion surface is unknown and potentially unbounded.

evidence_analysis.baseline_capture_status: Item 13.3 is present in the baseline capture (baseline items: 1.3, 9.1, 13.3). Source packets exist for all three items. Item 13.3 artifacts are present in baseline capture.
evidence_analysis.source_artifact_review: Source packets, intermediate JSON, and rendered documents exist for item 13.3 from baseline capture. Artifacts are structurally valid within the baseline context.
evidence_analysis.taxonomy_structure_review: Item 13.3 is a known valid item in the Ruankaodaren taxonomy and appears in the official exam syllabus. However, the expansion surface of 13.3 is suspected to be large or structurally ambiguous. Expansion might introduce multiple sub-items, internal multi-card topics, or boundary ambiguity with adjacent topics.

rationale: Item 13.3 is structurally valid in the baseline
However, its expansion surface is unknown and risky
Without a full taxonomy map, expanding 13.3 could introduce unbounded expansion
Expansion could create taxonomy drift or violate controlled expansion constraints
Conservative principle: exclude high-risk items until full taxonomy verification is complete
Item 13.3 may be reconsidered in a future phase after full taxonomy map is available and expansion boundaries are verified
quarantine_decision.quarantine_scope: All future expansion batches until taxonomy map is complete and verified

interpretation: The suspect status is resolved by quarantine

However, expansion is still blocked by other gates: phase6_1_entry_allowed: false, activation_allowed: false, batch_executable: false
Phase 6.2 resolves one blocker (taxonomy suspect recheck) but does NOT open execution gates
